item.title: Setting user permissions in KVM
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
In a preôÙviôÙous arôÙtiôÙcle I menôÙtioned how to inôÙstall a liôÙbrary in FeôÙdoôÙra, in orôÙder to make KVM virôÙtuôÙalôÙizaôÙtion easier, manôÙagôÙing the NAT netôÙwork conôÙfigôÙuôÙraôÙtion beôÙtween the guest virôÙtuôÙal ôÙmaôÙchine and the host, by means of libôÙvirt.

BeôÙsides that, while usôÙing KVM loôÙcalôÙly for deôÙvelôÙopôÙmenôÙt, I use virt-ôÙôÙmanôÙagôÙer, a helpôÙful apôÙpliôÙcaôÙtion that manôÙages the difôÙferôÙent virôÙtuôÙal ôÙmaôÙchines. This apôÙpliôÙcaôÙtion, as well as the rest of the comôÙmands that inôÙterôÙacôÙt with libôÙvirt (virsh for exôÙamôÙple), reôÙquire suôÙper usôÙer privôÙiôÙleges, so it will prompt for the suôÙdo passôÙword evôÙery time.

This can be avoidôÙed by inôÙcludôÙing the usôÙer inôÙto the folôÙlowôÙing groupôÙs: kvm, and libôÙvirt.

ThereôÙfore, just by runôÙning the folôÙlowôÙing comôÙmand we can skip the passôÙword prompt evôÙery time.

sudo usermod -a -G kvm,libvirt mariano

This is an opôÙtion I would use onôÙly for loôÙcal deôÙvelôÙopôÙment on my maôÙchine. ProôÙducôÙtive enôÙviôÙronôÙments must have an strict perôÙmisôÙsions manôÙageôÙmenôÙt.

item.title: Libvirt networking libraries
item.dc:creator: Mariano Anaya
item.description:
FeôÙdoôÙra 21 workôÙstaôÙtion seems to come with a lot of virôÙtuôÙalôÙizaôÙtion feaôÙtures and most of the libôÙvirt liôÙbraries inôÙstalled. I onôÙly had to add the KVM virôÙôÙtuôÙal-ôÙôÙmanôÙagôÙer which is the KVM apôÙpliôÙcaôÙtion I am more faôÙmilôÙiar with. HowôÙevôÙer, the new verôÙsion of the libôÙvirt* liôÙbraries have netôÙworkôÙing feaôÙtures that are great for the daôÙta cenôÙtre enôÙviôÙronôÙmenôÙt, but maybe not the best opôÙtion ôÙfor a parôÙticôÙuôÙlar workôÙstaôÙtion, so I added the folôÙlowôÙing packôÙages in orôÙder to set up an easôÙier netôÙwork conôÙfigôÙuôÙraôÙtion for my loôÙcal virôÙtuôÙal maôÙchines.

sudo dnf install virt-install libvirt-daemon-config-network

In orôÙder to reôÙflect the changes and start usôÙing the new feaôÙtures, we need to restart its serôÙvice:

systemctl restart libvirtd.service

AfôÙter that, when creôÙatôÙing a new virôÙtuôÙal maôÙchine, the NAT opôÙtion is enôÙabled, and the virôÙtuôÙal ôÙmanôÙagôÙer with hanôÙdle the NAT or bridgôÙing conôÙfigôÙuôÙraôÙtion auôÙtoôÙmatôÙiôÙcalôÙly, which alôÙlows me to deôÙploy new ôÙmaôÙchines faster.
